title_zh: 设计基于NVIDIA GQE的GPU加速查询引擎
title_en: Designing GPU-Accelerated Query Engines with NVIDIA GQE
summary_zh: GPU加速查询引擎通常受限于内存和I/O带宽。NVIDIA硬件的进步——包括高带宽内存（HBM）和NVIDIA的最新技术——推动了查询引擎性能的提升。
summary_en: GPU-accelerated query engines are often limited by memory and I/O bandwidth. Advances in NVIDIA hardware—including high bandwidth memory (HBM) and NVIDIA's latest technologies—are driving improvements in query engine performance.
why_it_matters_zh: 了解NVIDIA的硬件进展和GQE设计理念，有助于开发更高效的GPU查询引擎，突破性能瓶颈。
why_it_matters_en: Learning about NVIDIA's hardware advances and GQE design principles helps develop more efficient GPU query engines that overcome performance bottlenecks.

title_zh: NVIDIA BioNeMo代理工具包助力Claude Science中的生命科学研究者加速AI应用
title_en: NVIDIA BioNeMo Agent Toolkit Brings Accelerated AI to Life Sciences Researchers in Claude Science
summary_zh: 生命科学进入了计算规模时代。十多年来，NVIDIA构建了完整的GPU加速计算堆栈，包括硬件、框架、库、模型、微服务和领域专用工具，帮助研究人员运行更复杂的工作流程并更快迭代。近期，Anthropic发布了Claude Science，这是一个面向科学的AI工作平台。
summary_en: Life sciences have entered an era of computational scale. For over a decade, NVIDIA has developed a full GPU-accelerated computing stack—covering hardware, frameworks, libraries, models, microservices, and domain-specific tools—to help researchers run more sophisticated workflows and iterate faster. This week, Anthropic announced Claude Science, an AI workbench for science.
why_it_matters_zh: NVIDIA的BioNeMo代理工具包集成于Claude Science，利用其强大的GPU加速能力，使生命科学研究者能够更快速地进行复杂的AI计算和实验，提升科研效率。
why_it_matters_en: NVIDIA's BioNeMo Agent Toolkit integrates with Claude Science to leverage powerful GPU acceleration, enabling life sciences researchers to perform complex AI computations and experiments faster, thereby enhancing research efficiency.

title_zh: NVIDIA推理软件堆栈如何实现最低代币成本
title_en: How NVIDIA’s Inference Software Stack Powers the Lowest Token Cost
summary_zh: 随着组织从AI试点转向生产级AI工厂，基础设施的关注点从峰值芯片规格转向每代币成本：即每美元、每瓦特及满足延迟目标所能提供的有效代币数量。NVIDIA的推理软件堆栈与其GPU、CPU、网络和系统共同设计，结合广泛的开源生态系统，助力实现最低代币成本。
summary_en: As organizations transition from AI pilots to production AI factories, infrastructure decisions have shifted focus from peak chip specifications to cost per token: how many useful tokens can be delivered per dollar, per watt, and within latency requirements. NVIDIA’s inference software stack, co-designed with its GPUs, CPUs, networking, and systems, and supported by a broad open source ecosystem, enables the lowest token cost.
why_it_matters_zh: NVIDIA采用软硬件协同设计与开源生态的优势，优化算力与能耗的平衡，帮助企业降低AI推理的代币成本，提高经济效益。
why_it_matters_en: By leveraging co-design of hardware and software alongside a strong open source ecosystem, NVIDIA optimizes the balance between compute power and energy consumption, helping enterprises reduce AI inference token costs and improve cost efficiency.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: How NVIDIA’s Inference Software Stack Powers the Lowest Token Cost. Category: coding_ai. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.

summary_zh: Jaiveer Singh谈论机器人时，强调的是基础设施：机器内部的电路板、让开发者能通过机器人摄像头观察的软件下载，以及机器人离开演示场去完成实际任务所需的工程工作。他作为机器人士软件工程师，领导着相关团队。
summary_en: When Jaiveer Singh discusses robots, he focuses on infrastructure: the boards inside machines, software enabling developers to view through robots’ cameras, and the engineering needed before robots move from demos to real tasks. He leads the team behind this work as a robotics software engineer.

why_it_matters_en: Understanding the infrastructure behind robots helps accelerate development and deployment, pushing robotics technology towards practical use.

title_zh: 什么驱动了来自反馈的交互式改进？
title_en: What Drives Interactive Improvement from Feedback?
summary_zh: 本文研究了自然语言反馈如何在多轮交互中带来超越简单重复尝试的性能提升。通过设计一个学生-教师控制协议，评估了包括Omni-MATH、Codeforces在内的多个任务和十三个开源模型，比较了外部反馈、自我反馈和无指导自我改进。结果发现，多轮改进往往并不完全依赖反馈，学生实际利用反馈的能力比教师身份对提升效果影响更大。强有力的外部反馈能带来显著增益，表明有用反馈需提供超越重复尝试的指导。研究强调评估反馈型代理时应与重复尝试基线比较，并指出反馈执行能力是交互改进的关键瓶颈。
summary_en: This study investigates when natural-language feedback leads to improvements beyond mere repeated attempts in multi-turn language agent tasks. Using a controlled student-teacher protocol across diverse benchmarks and 13 open-source models, it compares external feedback, self-feedback, and unguided self-refinement. Findings reveal that multi-turn improvements often do not reflect effective feedback use; rather, the student’s capacity to leverage feedback predominantly drives gains, more than the teacher's identity. Strong external teachers generate substantially larger improvements, indicating useful feedback must guide beyond generic retries. The work suggests feedback-based agents should be benchmarked against repeated-attempt baselines and highlights that the ability to act on feedback is a central bottleneck for interactive performance enhancement.
why_it_matters_zh: 该研究通过严谨的控制实验揭示了交互式反馈改进的真正驱动力，不仅为理解多轮交流中反馈机制提供了新视角，也为开发更高效的反馈利用策略指明方向，尤其强调了反馈执行能力的重要性。
why_it_matters_en: This research offers a controlled and comprehensive analysis revealing that effective interactive improvement relies more on the student's ability to apply feedback rather than just feedback availability. It advances understanding of feedback mechanisms in multi-turn settings and guides future development of agents that better utilize feedback for enhanced performance.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: What Drives Interactive Improvement from Feedback?. Category: coding_ai. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.
ranking_reason: Selected from authoritative primary source; quality score 75.

title_zh: 法律领域的多智能体审议研究
summary_zh: 人工智能在法律领域的应用日益广泛，特别是基于大型语言模型（LLMs）的多智能体系统展现出自动执行操作的潜力。本文探讨了多智能体审议（MAD）方法处理法律推理任务，提出了两种受法庭程序和法律论证启发的新型多智能体框架。实验表明，多智能体框架在整体表现上与单一大型语言模型相当，但给出的答案显著不同，且能够解决基线模型无法处理的问题。定性分析显示，多智能体方法在从多个视角进行批判性思考的问题上表现更优。该研究展示了法律领域中多智能体系统的发展前景及其审议潜力。
summary_en: Artificial Intelligence is increasingly applied to law, especially agentic AI based on Large Language Models (LLMs) that can act autonomously. This paper investigates multi-agent deliberation (MAD) methods for legal reasoning tasks and introduces two novel frameworks inspired by courtroom procedures and legal argumentation. Experiments show multi-agent frameworks perform comparably to baseline LLMs but produce distinctly different answers, successfully solving cases the baseline fails. Qualitative evaluation highlights multi-agent approaches excel in questions requiring critical thinking from multiple perspectives. This work showcases the promising direction of multi-agent systems for AI in law and the potential of law-inspired deliberation methods.
why_it_matters_zh: 法律推理往往涉及复杂且多元的视角，多智能体系统能模拟法庭上的多方讨论，增强AI的推理深度和多样性，有助于提升司法领域AI的表现和公平性。
why_it_matters_en: Legal reasoning involves complex and diverse perspectives. Multi-agent systems can mimic courtroom deliberations, enhancing AI's depth and diversity in reasoning, thereby improving performance and fairness in legal AI applications.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: Investigating Multi-Agent Deliberation in Law. Category: research_papers. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.
ranking_reason: Selected from authoritative primary source; quality score 71.

title_zh: RoPoLL：鲁棒的大型语言模型评审小组
title_en: RoPoLL: Robust Panel of LLM Judges
summary_zh: 本文介绍了RoPoLL，一种基于鲁棒均值估计的LLM评审小组方法，针对现有基于多数共识的PoLL在存在偏见误判时表现出的无限偏差问题，RoPoLL使用几何中值替代简单平均，提高了对偏见和恶意攻击的鲁棒性。实验在13种不同规模模型、三类奖励模型基准和多种腐败模式下验证，RoPoLL显著优于传统PoLL，在部分场景下参数规模小得多却表现更佳，展示了其在偏倚污染条件下的实际应用价值。
summary_en: This paper presents RoPoLL, a robust panel method for large language model (LLM) evaluation that replaces the simple consensus aggregation in PoLL with a geometric median-based robust mean estimator. Under the Huber contamination model, RoPoLL overcomes the unbounded bias problems caused by biased, typical LLM failures such as mode collapse or sycophancy. Extensive experiments across 13 open-weight judges, multiple reward-model benchmarks, and various corruption scenarios demonstrate that RoPoLL outperforms PoLL consistently, especially under biased corruptions, even achieving better performance with significantly fewer parameters, validating its practical robustness and efficiency.
why_it_matters_zh: 评审多模型性能时，单模型误判带来的偏差难以避免。RoPoLL通过引入鲁棒统计方法，有效抵御了偏倚和恶意攻击，提高了评估的公平性和稳定性，对于LLM性能评测具有重要意义。
why_it_matters_en: Single-judge evaluation of LLMs is vulnerable to biased errors that lead to unbounded evaluation bias. RoPoLL applies robust statistical techniques, specifically the geometric median, to mitigate the impacts of biased or adversarial judges, ensuring more reliable and stable performance assessment. This advancement is critical for trustworthy LLM benchmarking.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: RoPoLL: Robust Panel of LLM Judges. Category: research_papers. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.
ranking_reason: Selected from authoritative primary source; quality score 76.

title_zh: 从命题不对称到感知不对称：将摩擦策略优化扩展至不对称部分信息对话
title_en: From Propositional to Perceptual Asymmetry: Extending Frictive Policy Optimization to Asymmetric Partial Information Dialogue
summary_zh: 本文将摩擦策略优化（FPO）方法从处理命题不对称扩展到感知不对称，即对话参与者拥有不对称的部分信息，导致同一指称表达在不同信息状态下含义不同。通过跨语料库分析和大型语言模型（LLM）探测，验证了FPO摩擦函数在各参与者视角内的有效性，并发现不同地标配置产生不同的语境构建失败模式。实验发现，单一“正确视角”胜过同时获得双方信息的全知视角。文章还提出了挂起语境构建状态的子类型分解和对齐分类的调节感知注释方法。
summary_en: This paper extends Frictive Policy Optimization (FPO) from propositional asymmetry to perceptual asymmetry in dialogue, where participants hold asymmetric partial information causing referential expressions to have different meanings depending on the information state. Through cross-corpora analysis and large language model probing mainly on the HCRC MapTask, it validates FPO's friction function within each participant's information horizon and reveals distinct grounding failure modes due to different landmark configurations. Results show that having the 'right perspective' outperforms omniscient access to both participants' contexts. The paper also proposes annotation refinements including subtype decomposition of pending grounding states and accommodation-aware alignment classification.
why_it_matters_zh: 本工作推动了对话中摩擦的认知理解，首次系统地研究了感知不对称情况下的语境构建问题，揭示了不同参与者信息视角对理解成功至关重要，且提出了注释方法升级，为对话系统设计和分析提供了新的理论和实证基础。
why_it_matters_en: This work advances the cognitive understanding of friction in dialogue by systematically addressing perceptual asymmetry in common-ground formation, highlighting the critical role of each participant's information perspective for successful understanding, and proposing annotation enhancements. It provides new theoretical and empirical insights that benefit dialogue system design and analysis.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: From Propositional to Perceptual Asymmetry: Extending Frictive Policy Optimization to Asymmetric Partial Information Dialogue. Category: research_papers. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.
ranking_reason: Selected from authoritative primary source; quality score 63.

title_zh: 基于参考的语调和节奏评估方法用于语音对话系统
title_en: Reference-Based Prosody and Rhythm Evaluation for Spoken Dialogue Systems
summary_zh: 本文提出了一种利用超过4000小时的英语对话数据，从音高平均值、音高表现力、语速、发音速率、停顿比例和平均停顿时长等指标，构建匹配参考体系的语调和节奏评估协议。通过对比语音生成系统输出与最接近的人类参考层级，报告百分位偏差或范围外标记，实现了对对话系统语音表现的更准确评估。该方法可作为行为合理性检测，补充传统的感知和用户评估。
summary_en: This paper introduces a percentile-based evaluation protocol for spoken dialogue systems' prosody and rhythm using over 4000 hours of English dyadic conversations. Metrics such as mean and expressivity of fundamental frequency (F_0), speech and articulation rates, pause ratio, and mean pause duration are compared between system outputs and matched human reference strata. This approach improves calibration over pooled human statistics, allowing interpretable deviation reporting and serving as behavioral plausibility checks complementary to perceptual evaluation.
why_it_matters_zh: 现有评估方法因未考虑说话者特质和交互状态的影响，导致评估结果难以解释和校准。该研究通过构建匹配的人类参考参考体系，提升了评估的准确性和解释性，有助于推动语音对话系统的质量提升。
why_it_matters_en: Existing evaluation approaches struggle with calibration and interpretability due to speaker traits and interaction state variability. This work's matched reference framework enhances accuracy and interpretability of prosody and rhythm evaluations, thereby advancing the development of higher-quality spoken dialogue systems.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: Reference-Based Prosody and Rhythm Evaluation for Spoken Dialogue Systems. Category: research_papers. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.
ranking_reason: Selected from authoritative primary source; quality score 67.

title_zh: 构建学术论文的多模态关键词提取数据集
title_en: Building a Multimodal Dataset of Academic Paper for Keyword Extraction
summary_zh: 目前关键词提取任务主要依赖文本数据，忽视了图像和音频模态的视觉细节和音频特征，导致信息丰富度不足和潜在关联被忽视，限制了模型表征学习和预测准确性。现有多模态关键词提取数据集稀缺，阻碍研究进展。该研究构建了包含论文文本、图像、音频和关键词的1000样本多模态学术论文数据集，并基于无监督和有监督方法进行实验，探索不同模态及其融合在关键词提取任务中的表现差异。结果显示，来自不同模态的文本具有不同特征，论文文本、图像文本和音频文本的结合有效提升了关键词提取性能。
summary_en: Keyword extraction tasks have largely relied solely on textual data, overlooking visual and audio modality features, which limits information richness and model learning capacity. Existing multimodal datasets for this task are scarce, hindering research progress. This study builds a multimodal academic paper dataset with 1000 samples, each including text, images, audios, and keywords. Experiments using unsupervised and supervised methods explore the different performances of keyword extraction based on paper text, text from images, and audio. Results indicate distinct characteristics across modalities, and combining paper text, image text, and audio text effectively improves keyword extraction performance.
why_it_matters_zh: 多模态信息整合解决了单一文本信息的不足，提升了关键词提取的准确性和模型性能，推动了包含多种信息源的智能分析技术发展。
why_it_matters_en: Integrating multimodal information addresses the limitations of relying solely on text, enhancing accuracy and model performance in keyword extraction, and advancing intelligent analysis techniques that leverage diverse data sources.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: Building a Multimodal Dataset of Academic Paper for Keyword Extraction. Category: image_ai. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.
ranking_reason: Selected from authoritative primary source; quality score 66.

title_zh: DiScoFormer：一个处理密度和评分的通用变换器，适用于多种分布
title_en: DiScoFormer: One transformer for density and score, across distributions
summary_zh: DiScoFormer是一种变换器模型，能够同时处理数据密度估计和评分，适用于不同数据分布。它为多分布任务提供统一高效的解决方案。
summary_en: DiScoFormer is a transformer model capable of simultaneously handling density estimation and scoring across different data distributions. It provides a unified and efficient approach for tasks involving multiple distributions.
why_it_matters_zh: DiScoFormer的多功能特性简化了跨分布数据的处理流程，提升了建模效率和精度，对机器学习和统计分析有重要意义。
why_it_matters_en: DiScoFormer's multifunctionality simplifies the handling of cross-distribution data, improving modeling efficiency and accuracy, which is significant for machine learning and statistical analysis.
